creator: fabian@wenks.ch
text:
(In reply to Miroslav Lachman from comment #11)

Of course I did restart the daemon (Sendmail), as I am not using saslauth and directly using SASL with SQL. Restarting the daemon worked fine with SpamAssassin and it was using the new socket. No idea what magic Perl has.

Thank you for looking into it. For my setup it is fine with localhost:3306, but this may be different for larger installations.

Hopefully some of this discoveries we have so far can be resolved until MariaDB 10.6 is promoted as default version, else it may need a larger entry in UPDATING and also something when the port / pkg is installed.
